title: NOC 92024 – Supervisors, other products manufacturing and assembly | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors under NOC 92024 are the versatile leaders of Canada’s "Miscellaneous Manufacturing" sector. This TEER 2 occupation is a broad category covering the production of a wide range of essential goods that do not fall under traditional metal, wood, or electronics categories—including jewelry, sporting goods, toys, musical instruments, and medical dental appliances. In 2026, as Canada emphasizes "Niche Manufacturing" and high-value consumer goods, these supervisors are critical for managing specialized assembly lines and artisanal-to-industrial transitions. For immigration, this role is a strong candidate for the OINP Employer Job Offer stream and the Federal Skilled Trades Program (FSTP).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Level2Major Group92 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsSub-Major Group920 – Processing, manufacturing and utilities supervisorsMinor Group9202 – Supervisors, assembly and fabricationNOC Code92024

duties: Supervision: Coordinate and schedule the activities of workers who assemble, finish, and inspect various consumer and industrial products.
Operational Strategy: Establish methods to meet strict work schedules and coordinate activities with design, procurement, and logistics departments.
Quality Assurance: Resolve technical work problems and recommend measures to improve productivity and final product aesthetics/functionality.
Resource Management: Requisition materials and supplies (e.g., precious metals, specialized polymers, or medical-grade resins).
Staff Development: Train workers in job duties, precision hand-tool usage, and safety procedures (including chemical handling).
Compliance: Ensure strict adherence to safety rules, environmental regulations, and specific industry quality standards (e.g., ISO 13485 for medical devices).
Reporting: Prepare detailed production logs, safety audit reports, and material waste summaries for plant management.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is mandatory.
Work Experience: Several years of experience in the specific assembly or manufacturing occupation supervised are required (e.g., as a jeweler, dental technician, or assembler).
Education: A college diploma or vocational training in industrial management, manufacturing technology, or a field related to the specific product (e.g., dental technology) is often required.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in Lean Manufacturing, Quality Management Systems (QMS), and basic ERP software.
Strong leadership, attention to detail, and the ability to manage diverse production processes are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, supervisors in high-tech sectors like Medical/Dental Device Fabrication often command a 15–20% premium above the median industrial wage.
job_outlook: The outlook is Stable. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), the sector will face a consistent need for new supervisors due to the retirement of current plant managers. While some mass-production has moved offshore, Canada’s growth in high-value, specialized assembly (e.g., custom medical appliances and luxury sporting equipment) ensures a steady demand for skilled production leaders.
labour_market_demand: The demand for supervisors in other products manufacturing is Good, with a high concentration in Canada’s urban manufacturing zones. In 2026, Ontario (GTA) and Quebec (Montreal) are the primary hiring hubs for specialized sectors like Medical Device Assembly and Commercial Sporting Goods. With the rise of "Personalized Manufacturing" (3D printing and custom assembly), there is a growing need for supervisors who can manage flexible production schedules and multi-skilled assembly teams.
Diverse Industry Exposure: Lead production teams across unique sub-sectors, from high-end jewelry and musical instruments to critical healthcare products.
Technological Integration: Manage modern facilities utilizing Additive Manufacturing (3D Printing) and digital inventory management systems.
Stable Economic Niche: Many "miscellaneous" manufacturers in Canada focus on high-margin, specialized exports that remain resilient during economic shifts.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 2 role is a recognized "Skilled Worker" category for Express Entry and various provincial "In-Demand" lists.
pathways: Express Entry (Category-Based Selection): Potential targeting under the "Trade" category or specific provincial "General Skilled Worker" draws.
OINP Employer Job Offer (Foreign Worker Stream): A direct route for those with a job offer from an Ontario-based miscellaneous manufacturer.
Manitoba PNP (Skilled Worker Stream): Often targets supervisors for the province's diverse manufacturing cluster.
BC PNP Skills Immigration: Priority for supervisors supporting the province’s specialized manufacturing and design sectors.
